## Corpora Considered

Machine-readable catalog: [plot3_preregistered_source_catalog.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot3_preregistered_source_catalog.csv)

At the moment Plot 3 has `9` included source families and `31` explicitly considered but not included source families.

This table is the corpus-level admission audit: what was considered, why it looked eligible, what fields were actually confirmed, why it is in or out, and how many observations made it into the plotted layer.

::: {.dataset-table .source-audit-table .plot3-source-audit-table}

| Corpus considered | What it is / why possible candidate | Confirmed fields | Why in/out | # obs in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | Preregistered psychology-article key-question effects from Schäfer/Schwarz. Possible Plot 3 candidate because the public coded sample separates preregistered from non-preregistered articles and already provides N plus a converted r/d effect for each usable preregistered key effect. | Known: 93 preregistered coded rows, 89 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration flag: yes (93); paper grouping: yes (93); paper D/N: yes (89); support status: not coded. | Included: 89 preregistered key-effect rows enter as support-not-coded preregistered results. The 4 remaining coded rows lack usable positive D/N. | 89 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Published psychology Registered Reports coded by Scheel et al. as preregistered hypotheses. Possible Plot 3 candidate because each row is an analytically preregistered confirmatory hypothesis and the local appendix table recovers D/N for the extractable subset. | Known: 71 preregistered hypothesis rows, 32 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes (71); support status: yes (32); journal provenance: yes (32); paper D/N: yes (32). New quote-stat rescue queue: 13 candidate statistic rows, 9 with candidate N, all marked not strict-ready pending PDF/focal checks. | Included: 32 hypotheses enter. The remaining 39 Scheel rows are real preregistered hypotheses. The local quote-stat pass now identifies 13 rescue candidates, but they remain outside strict Plot 3 until their article PDFs verify the exact first-hypothesis row and analytic N. | 32 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated papers [@tyner2026] | The SCORE/COS claim corpus was rechecked because its public OSF files expose separate original-study effect sizes, effective-N fields, and `orig_prereg-indicated.csv`; the later SCORE replicability package at OSF g5sny also exposes an `analyst data.RData` object with original and replication outcome tables. The local SCORE D/N parser already reconstructs paper-level D/N for a subset of preregistration-indicated papers. | Known: 245 unique SCORE paper IDs marked `prereg=True`, 31 deduplicated paper-level D/N rows. Confirmed: paper-level preregistration indication: yes (245); public flat payloads: `orig_effect-size.csv`, `orig_stats_effective.csv`, and `orig_prereg-indicated.csv`; g5sny package: yes, with 825 original-outcome rows across 306 papers and 427 replication-outcome rows; paper grouping: yes; D/N: yes (31); claim-specific preregistration mapping: no; support status: not coded. | Included: 31 paper-level rows enter. The remaining 214 preregistration-indicated SCORE paper IDs do not currently have a deduplicated positive D/N row in the local SCORE candidate build. Caveat: the public files are split across claim-level effect sizes, claim-level effective-N rows, paper-level preregistration flags, and g5sny RData objects, so the table does not assert that every extracted claim was the exact preregistered primary hypothesis. | 31 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | PSA-CR002 was considered because the article explicitly labels Table 2 as effect sizes and statistics for each preregistered hypothesis, while Table 3 reports the relevant arm sample sizes after preregistered exclusions. | Known: 12 Table 2 preregistered hypothesis rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes (12); source focal selector: Table 2 preregistered hypothesis rows; reported Cohen's d: yes (12); arm/sample N: yes from Table 3 (12); support status: yes. | Included: 12 preregistered PSA-CR002 Table 2 hypotheses enter. The signs of the reported effects are hypothesis-direction-coded in the article; the plotted D axis uses magnitudes. | 12 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | PSA-CR001 was a preregistered 52-country COVID-19 framing project. Possible Plot 3 candidate because four primary confirmatory outcomes can be represented as pooled D/N rows rather than as nested country cells. | Known: 4 pooled primary outcomes. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes (4); support status: yes (4); pooled N: yes (4); pooled D: yes (4). | Included: the four primary PSA-CR001 confirmatory outcomes enter as pooled rows. Country-level cells stay collapsed here to avoid nested non-independent Plot 3 rows. | 4 |
| ManyBabies 1 bilingual IDS pooled row [@byersHeinlein2021manybabiesBilingual] | ManyBabies 1 Bilingual was considered because it is the preregistered companion project to ManyBabies 1 and reports the bilingual-infant pooled IDS-versus-ADS preference effect with a standardized within-infant effect size. | Known: one pooled primary bilingual IDS-versus-ADS effect. Confirmed locally from the public analysis repository: preregistered many-lab project: yes; focal pooled bilingual effect: yes; usable bilingual full-dataset N: 324 infants; reported full-dataset bilingual dz: 0.26; Plot 1 duplicate check: no local ManyBabies 1 Bilingual pair row found. | Included: one pooled ManyBabies 1 Bilingual infant-directed-speech preference row enters. The monolingual comparison rows and site/moderator rows are deliberately not added to avoid nested non-independent Plot 3 rows. | 1 |
| ManyBabies 1 IDS pooled row [@manyBabies2020Ids] | ManyBabies 1 was considered because the project page documents a large preregistered many-lab infant study and the AMPPS article reports the pooled primary infant-directed-speech preference effect with N and Cohen's d. | Known: one pooled primary IDS-versus-ADS effect. Confirmed: preregistered many-lab project: yes; focal pooled primary effect: yes; N: 2,329 babies tested; reported Cohen's d: 0.35; Plot 1 duplicate check: no local ManyBabies 1 pair row found. | Included: one pooled ManyBabies 1 infant-directed-speech preference row enters. Lab, method, age, and language moderator rows are deliberately not added to avoid nested non-independent Plot 3 rows. | 1 |
| ManyClasses 1 feedback pooled row [@fyfe2021manyclasses1] | ManyClasses 1 was considered because the article documents a time-stamped preregistration, public materials/data/scripts, and a single pooled primary classroom-learning estimate for immediate versus delayed feedback timing. | Known: one pooled primary feedback-timing effect across 38 classes. Confirmed: preregistration: yes (OSF registration dated July 22, 2019); source focal selector: overall feedback-timing estimate; N: 2,081 participating students; standardized pooled effect: Δz = 0.002 with 95% HDI [-0.05, 0.05]; support status: no. | Included: one pooled ManyClasses 1 immediate-versus-delayed-feedback row enters. Class-level estimates and moderator rows are deliberately not added to avoid nested non-independent Plot 3 rows. | 1 |
| PSA004 true-belief/Gettier pooled row [@hall2024psa004Turri] | PSA004 was considered because the public PSA project page and Hall et al. article report a pooled preregistered cross-cultural contrast for standard justified true belief versus Gettier-type cases, with N and an odds ratio. | Known: one pooled standard JTB-versus-Gettier project effect. Confirmed: preregistered PSA project: yes; focal pooled effect: yes; N: 4,724; OR: 1.86; D conversion: yes via Chinn log-OR-to-d. | Included: one pooled PSA004 standard JTB-versus-Gettier row enters. This is related to, but not identical with, the Darrel-vignette PSA004 Plot 1 replication-pair rescue; the source catalog flags the cross-plot overlap risk. | 1 |
| AACT x PubMed registered primary outcomes [@du2024] | ClinicalTrials.gov/AACT primary-outcome linkages were considered because trial registration can identify focal biomedical outcomes at scale. | Known: 2,720 registered primary-outcome rows in the earlier audit. Confirmed: trial registration: yes; analytic preregistration plan: no/insufficient; local Plot 3 D/N result rows: no. | Not included: registry metadata can support a comparator layer, but these rows are not yet analytic-preregistration result rows on the shared D/N axis. | 0 |
| Allen/Mehler RR support-rate review [@allenMehler2019OpenScience] | Allen and Mehler looked like a Plot 3 rescue source because the article surveyed published biomedical and psychological Registered Reports and counted clearly stated a priori hypotheses. | Known from the article: 127 published RRs were surveyed, 113 were included, and the authors extracted 296 a priori hypotheses (153 replication-attempt hypotheses and 143 original-research hypotheses). Confirmed: Registered Report provenance: yes; support/null status: yes; exact D/N result statistics: no. | Not included: this is useful RR-universe evidence and overlaps conceptually with Scheel, but the public article/supporting data count supported versus unsupported hypotheses rather than exposing the exact statistic, effect size, and analytic N for each hypothesis. | 0 |
| Brodeur preregistered/PAP table tests [@brodeur2020] | The Brodeur economics corpus was rechecked because it is the other local source that produces thousands of preregistration-flagged D/N rows: many extracted table tests are from studies marked as preregistered or having a pre-analysis plan. | Known locally: 10,071 extracted economics test rows, 3,724 D/N-ready rows with preregistration/PAP/PAP-power flags, collapsing to 70 papers. Confirmed: preregistration flag rows: 3,547; pre-analysis-plan flag rows: 2,006; PAP-power flag rows: 1,720; D/N: yes; focal/main-result selector: no; exact test-level PAP mapping: no. | Not included: the preregistration/PAP flags are real and account for 3,724 D/N-ready extracted table-test rows, but the flags are not enough to prove that each coefficient/test is the exact prespecified confirmatory hypothesis. No focal/main-result selector is available, so this remains a sensitivity lane rather than strict Plot 3 evidence. | 0 |
| COMPare prespecified outcome audit | COMPare looked like a missing Plot 3 source because the local parser recovers outcome-level rows and explicitly separates prespecified primary outcomes, prespecified secondary outcomes, and non-prespecified published outcomes. | Known locally: 72 trial reports and 1,171 outcome rows: 89 prespecified primary, 660 prespecified secondary, 422 non-prespecified publication outcomes. Confirmed: prespecified-outcome status: yes; trial/publication provenance: yes; effect estimate and N fields: no. | Not included: the prespecified-outcome labels are exactly the kind of audit trail we wanted to check, but the parsed rows stop at outcome text/reporting status. There is no D/N layer to plot. | 0 |
| CliniFact published trial primary-outcome rows [@zhang2025clinifact] | CliniFact was rechecked because these rows are journal-side clinical trial primary-outcome proxies with D/N on disk, so they are closer to published preregistered work than raw registry rows. | Known locally: 339 published trial primary-outcome rows, 339 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes; primary-outcome registry linkage: yes; D/N: yes; article-level analytic preregistration/hypothesis text: not confirmed. | Not included here: 339 D/N rows exist and are already represented in the published-paper endpoint surface. The current Plot 3 gate requires explicit analytic preregistered confirmatory rows, not only registry-linked primary outcomes. | 0 |
| ClinicalTrials.gov registry-result D/N comparator [@du2024] | ClinicalTrials.gov results were rechecked because they are the largest local preregistered-like bucket: trial records have registered outcomes and the parser recovers N plus p-value/effect-size proxies. A cleaner sub-sidecar now isolates phase-2+ randomized interventional trials with exactly one locally eligible primary two-group row. | Known locally: broad sidecar has 7,722 trial-median registry rows and 7,722 D/N-ready rows. Cleaner local sub-sidecar has 3,149 phase-2+ one-primary randomized rows. Confirmed: outcome type: yes in raw file; study type/allocation/phase: yes in raw file; N/effect proxy: yes; direct arm-level D: no; protocol/SAP timing audit: no. | Not included in strict Plot 3: 7,722 broad D/N rows and 3,149 cleaner one-primary randomized rows are real and local, but the current D is a registry p-value/enrollment proxy and no protocol/SAP pre-measurement audit proves exact analytic prespecification. The cleaner subset remains a named sensitivity sidecar. | 0 |
| Communication privacy preregistered corpus [@masur2025privacy] | The communication privacy corpus was rechecked because it has an explicit preregistration PDF, local cleaned data, and many SEM/path outputs in the replication-pair file. | Known locally: 31 promoted/staged path rows and 8 downloaded source files. Confirmed: preregistration: yes; D/N or path-like rows: yes in Plot 1 context; standalone confirmatory result table for Plot 3: no; metric conversion policy: unresolved. | Not included: the preregistered corpus is real and local, but adding the path rows here would mix SEM/path metrics with the D/N confirmatory-result layer and double-count Plot 1-adjacent evidence. | 0 |
| EGAP Metaketa I native ATE rows [@metaketaIRepo] | Metaketa I was rechecked because the local GitHub archive contains the CUP Book chapter 11 meta-analysis scripts and tables for the preregistered information/accountability experiments. The pooled primary vote-choice estimate is a strong PAP/native-metric candidate. | Verified locally in `tab_11.1_main_effects.tex`: the overall vote-choice treatment estimate is 0.003 with SE 0.010, 25,820 observations, and control mean 0.369; good-news and bad-news vote-choice columns report 13,196 and 12,531 observations. Confirmed: coordinated PAP/native primary outcome: yes; N/observations and SE: yes; source-provided standardized D: no. | Not included: EGAP Metaketa I is a clean preregistered/PAP field-experiment source, but the local table is a covariate-adjusted linear-probability ATE with clustered SEs and nontrivial row construction, including Uganda 2 duplicate office-level observations. It should be staged as native ATE evidence unless a defensible standardized-D conversion is separately documented. | 0 |
| EGAP Metaketa III native rows [@metaketaIIIProject] | Metaketa III was recovered from the crash notes because the local staged harvest already documents public parent and component replication packages with site-estimate RData and Stata/R payloads. | Known locally: 30 expected native field-experiment rows and 26 downloaded files. Confirmed: public field-experiment replication package: yes; native ATE/site-estimate files: yes; source-provided standardized D/N row table: no. | Not included: Metaketa III is a strong PAP/coordinated-trial source, but the local payload is native site-estimate and field-experiment output. It belongs in a native metric side lane unless a source-defensible D conversion and one-row-per-study rule are added. | 0 |
| EGAP Metaketa IV native rows [@metaketaIVProject] | Metaketa IV was recovered from the crash notes because the local staged harvest includes an integrated OSF results tree with study-estimate and meta-estimate RDS outputs. | Known locally: 36 expected native field-experiment rows and 21 downloaded files. Confirmed: public field-experiment result outputs: yes; native study/meta estimates: yes; source-provided standardized D/N row table: no. | Not included: Metaketa IV is a strong PAP/coordinated-trial source, but the local results are native study and meta estimates. It should remain staged until a field-experiment native-metric lane or defensible D conversion policy exists. | 0 |
| ERN/Pe RRR OpenNeuro/OSF hubs [@ernPeRrrOpenNeuro] | ERN/Pe looked eligible because it is a Registered Replication Report with public OpenNeuro raw EEG and OSF analysis artifacts. | Known: OpenNeuro raw EEG plus OSF summary/analysis artifacts are local. Confirmed: preregistered replication status: yes; replication-side native metrics: yes; compact original-vs-replication D/N row table: no. | Not included: source is real and local, but it lacks a compact D/N table and the original-side result is missing from the local artifact set. | 0 |
| FORRT FReD / Replication Database | FReD was rechecked because it is a large replication database and the local clean export contributes many D/N rows to the replication and published-result layers. | Known locally: 843 FReD pair rows in the replication-pair file and 796 original published-target D/N rows in the published corpus. Confirmed: D/N: yes; original-replication pairing: yes; analytic preregistration flag for each source result: no. | Not included: FReD is already represented through Plot 1 pair evidence and Plot 2 original-target published evidence. The clean export does not make those rows standalone preregistered confirmatory results. | 0 |
| FReD archived workflow rescue queue | The FReD archive was rechecked because the freshness report found a possible rescue queue, including OSF Registries, ML1, RRR, Boyce, submissions, and OpenMKT-like sources. | Known locally: 343 converted/N-complete archive rows absent from the current clean export. A follow-up archive scan found 425 validated complete non-excluded rows overall, including 15 rows sourced from OSF Registries and 107 rows with a nonempty replication-preregistration field. Confirmed: N/effect conversion: partial; clean current-export status: no; duplicate/workflow filtering: unresolved; analytic preregistration: mixed/not confirmed. | Not included: the archive does contain preregistration-linked replication rows, including 15 OSF Registries rows, but they are replication-pair evidence rather than standalone Plot 3 result rows. Promoting them requires duplicate handling against existing FReD, Many Labs, RRR, and OSF-registries rows. | 0 |
| Linden focal/random psychology sample [@linden2024publicationBiasPsychology] | Linden was rechecked because the raw OSF materials include a preregistration artifact and the parsed focal/random effect rows are D/N-ready. | Known locally: 158 focal D/N rows and 158 random-effect D/N rows. Confirmed: D/N: yes (316); preregistration applies to the meta-research coding study, not to the sampled article findings. | Not included: the focal rows remain in the published-result layer and the random rows remain a comparator. The OSF preregistration does not make the sampled source articles analytically preregistered confirmatory results. | 0 |
| Many Labs 1-5 project-level replication rows | Many Labs 1-5 looked like Plot 3 candidates because the projects are coordinated large-sample replication efforts with preregistered or protocol-locked analyses and local D/N rows. | Known locally: 69 project-level D/N pair rows across Many Labs 1-5 in the replication-pair file. Confirmed: replication protocol status: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; independent single-result Plot 3 table: no. | Not included: local D/N rows exist, but they are already consumed by the replication-pair plot. Adding them here would double-count replication-side evidence unless Plot 3 gets a separate multi-lab-replication lane. | 0 |
| ManyBabies 3 registered-report materials [@manyBabies3Osf] | ManyBabies 3 looked eligible because it is a preregistered infant rule-learning multi-lab project. The repo has the OSF registered-report PDFs and public GitHub materials cached. | Known: target project expected roughly 15 lab/result rows in the harvest worklist. Confirmed: preregistered project materials: yes; final machine-readable site-level D/N result payload: no; promoted rows: 0. | Not included: preregistered materials are local, but the checked OSF archive and public GitHub repository did not expose final machine-readable infant result rows. | 0 |
| ManyBabies 4 helpers/hinderers RR materials [@manyBabies4Project] | ManyBabies 4 looked eligible because it is a Stage 1 and Stage 2 Registered Report, reports N tested and N included, and exposes public analysis/data files. It is a plausible one-row pooled primary-result rescue. | Known from the research pass: N tested = 1,018, N included = 567, public analysis repo names clean_data.csv and analysis qmd files. Confirmed: Registered Report status: yes; final machine-readable data likely public; exact Stage 1 focal contrast and exact denominators: not yet locally verified. | Not included yet: this is a strong near-row, but the current repo has not verified the Stage 1 focal contrast or exact counts from clean_data.csv. Rounded abstract percentages are not enough for strict Plot 3. | 0 |
| ManyClasses 2 classroom materials [@manyClasses2Osf] | ManyClasses 2 was rechecked because it is a coordinated preregistered classroom project with public OSF materials and a reported pooled prequestion-learning effect. The surfaced confirmatory result is a hierarchical log-odds/OR learning effect rather than a direct standardized D row. | Known locally: 30 expected rows, 12 downloaded files. Confirmed: public OSF payload: yes; Terracotta/classroom data and scripts: yes; N reported in the literature as 1,571; pooled log-odds/OR result surfaced; direct D/N confirmatory result table: no. | Not included: the best surfaced row is native log-odds/OR from a hierarchical classroom design. It stays staged until the project has an explicit, defensible OR-to-D policy and one-row-per-project extraction. | 0 |
| Nordic trial registration-publication linkage | The Nordic trial-reporting database looked like the missing large clinical-trial preregistration source because it has thousands of trial-registration to publication links and records whether publications matched the registered design, population, intervention, comparator, and any outcome. | Known locally: 7,461 registry-publication search rows, 5,660 journal-publication rows. Confirmed: trial registration IDs: yes; publication DOI/PMID: yes where found; outcome/effect/N fields for D conversion: no. | Not included: the rows confirm publication linkage and matching metadata, but they do not contain exact result statistics or D/N fields for the preregistered point-estimate plot. | 0 |
| High-Replicability Research project [@protzko2024highrep] | High-Replicability Research was considered because it was designed as a preregistered counter-pattern source with many planned high-replicability findings. | Known: 48 project findings claimed in the audit trail; 3 local D/N pair rows recovered in the replication-harvest sidecar. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes; D/N: partial (3); non-retracted status: no. | Not included: the source fails the non-retraction gate, so even locally recovered rows stay out of Plot 3. | 0 |
| PSA replication-pair rows | PSA projects looked eligible because they are preregistered, multi-site confirmatory projects. The local repo has PSA-002, PSA-004, and PSA-006 D/N pair rows. | Known locally: 93 PSA D/N pair rows (PSA-002 object orientation, PSA-004 Turri, PSA-006 trolley) in the replication-pair file. Confirmed: preregistered/multi-site status: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; standalone non-pair Plot 3 table: no. | Not included: the PSA pair rows are already part of the replication-pair evidence. PSA-CR001 is the exception because it contributes four pooled preregistered confirmatory outcomes without an original-vs-replication arrow. | 0 |
| RPCB Registered Report replication effects [@errington2021] | RPCB was rechecked because its replication protocols were submitted as Registered Reports before experimental work and the public OSF effect-level table contains replication sample sizes and SMD-like effect-size columns. | Known locally: 188 effect-level rows in the RPCB final effect table and 118 RPCB rows already in the replication-pair file. Confirmed from local table: replication sample size is present for 188 effects; replication SMD effect size is present for 159 effects; original sample size is numeric for 140 effects; original SMD effect size is numeric for 135 effects. A conservative first-effect-per-paper preclinical candidate file now contains 23 row-ready D/N candidates. Confirmed: Registered Report replication protocol status: yes; standalone non-overlap with Plot 1: no. | Not included in the psychology/social-science strict layer: RPCB is a real Registered Report replication source and now has 23 conservative paper-level D/N candidates, but adding them here would double-count RPCB against Plot 1 and mix preclinical replication effects with standalone confirmatory psychology/social-science rows. The candidate file is ready for a separate preclinical layer decision. | 0 |
| Registered Replication Reports Plot 1 pair rows | The live RRR rows looked like Plot 3 candidates because they come from preregistered replication reports and have local D/N encodings. | Known locally: 49 RRR/FReD-RRR D/N pair rows in the replication-pair file. Confirmed: preregistered replication status: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; independence from Plot 1: no. | Not included: the rows are real preregistered replication evidence with D/N, but their current role is original-vs-replication comparison. Plot 3 is reserved for standalone preregistered confirmatory result rows. | 0 |
| Registered Replication Reports per-lab rows | The RRR lab-level audit was considered because these are preregistered multi-lab replication analyses and the appendix documents recoverable per-lab D/N-style rows. | Known: 205 verified per-lab rows documented in the appendix across 10 RRRs. Confirmed: preregistered replication status: yes; current dedicated Plot 3 local source table: no; independence from Plot 1 pair rows: no. | Not included: real preregistered replication evidence, but adding lab rows here would double-count replication evidence already represented in Plot 1 until a separate within-project heterogeneity policy is written. | 0 |
| Retrieval-extinction rats preregistered replication [@luyten2017retrievalExtinction] | This source was rechecked because it is explicitly preregistered and the local harvest already promoted three original-vs-replication outcome rows. | Known locally: 3 D/N pair rows. Confirmed: preregistered replication: yes; D/N: yes; independent standalone Plot 3 row family: no. | Not included: the three rows are valid and already handled as original-vs-replication evidence. Plot 3 excludes them to avoid double-counting replication-pair arrows. | 0 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz non-preregistered comparator [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | The non-preregistered arm of Schäfer/Schwarz is useful as a within-source comparator because it has the same coded key-question-effect structure as the preregistered sample. | Known: 900 coded non-preregistered rows, 682 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: no; paper grouping: yes (900); paper D/N: yes (682); support status: not coded. | Not included: 682 D/N-ready rows exist and are already eligible for the published-paper endpoint surface, but the rows fail the analytic-preregistration gate for Plot 3. | 0 |
| Self-control fMRI preregistered materials | The self-control fMRI replication looked eligible because the OSF node contains preregistration/scripts and the paper/OpenNeuro materials are local. | Known: four expected candidate rows in the harvest worklist. Confirmed: preregistration materials: yes; raw fMRI/paper mirror: yes; machine-readable D/N result table: no; native neuroimaging metrics: unresolved. | Not included: the source is documented and useful, but the current local payload does not provide Plot 3-ready confirmatory D/N rows. | 0 |
| Transparent Psi Project / Bem row [@kekecs2023tpp] | The Transparent Psi Project looked eligible because it is explicitly preregistered and the local combined raw data promoted one first-stopping-point D/N row. | Known locally: 1 promoted D/N pair row. Confirmed: preregistered replication: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; standalone confirmatory-result source independent of Plot 1: no. | Not included: this is a clean preregistered replication row, but its current analytic role is a shrinkage arrow against Bem's original result, not a standalone preregistered-result observation. | 0 |
| Twomey kinesiology article audit | Twomey looked eligible because its public article-level table includes a preregistration flag and sample size for 300 recent kinesiology articles. | Known: 300 article rows, 27 marked preregistered, 295 with sample size. Confirmed: article grouping: yes; preregistration flag: yes; exact effect/test statistic for D conversion: no. | Not included: this is a real public preregistration-aware article audit, but the released p-value fields are reporting-format/threshold metadata rather than exact numeric statistics for D/N reconstruction. | 0 |
| van den Akker preregistration-in-practice corpus [@vanDenAkkerPreregPractice] | This corpus looked high-yield because the public OSF project exposes a 193-row preregistered-versus-non-preregistered publication table, parsed original-statistic spreadsheets, and selective-hypothesis-reporting coding sheets that can drive a later row-rescue pass. | Verified from the OSF API/downloads: `PvNP Data.xlsx` has 193 publication rows, `Original Stats.xlsx` has 640 parsed original-stat rows across 180 PSP IDs, `Control Stats.xlsx` holds the matched control-stat sample, and `Data SHR.xlsx` exposes hypothesis/reporting match fields. The local rescue parser now produces 124 first-convertible-statistic PSP candidates with D and N, all marked staged because the focal-hypothesis selector, relevance flags, metric conversion, and DOI deduplication are not yet strict-row audited. | Not included yet: the OSF payload is real and high-yield, and the local parser now finds 124 D/N candidates. They are still not automatically the first preregistered confirmatory hypothesis, so promotion still needs field verification, t/F/r-to-D conversion checks, and DOI-level deduplication against existing Schäfer/Schwarz, Scheel, SCORE, PSA, ManyBabies, and ManyClasses rows. | 0 |
| van den Akker selective-hypothesis-reporting corpus [@vanDenAkkerSelectiveHypothesis2023] | The selective-hypothesis-reporting corpus was separated from the 193-study preregistration-in-practice sample because the paper and local workbook expose a much larger study/hypothesis matching surface that can drive a later strict row-rescue pass. | Known from the article: 459 preregistered studies and 2,119 identified hypotheses. Verified locally in `Data SHR.xlsx`: 484 PSP IDs total, 446 PSP IDs marked `Inclusion=Yes`, 5,723 filled preregistered-hypothesis cells among included PSP IDs, and 3,342 publication-match cells beginning with `Yes`. Confirmed: preregistration/publication hypothesis matching: yes; support/match status: yes; source-provided D/N: no. | Not included yet: this is the strongest large-volume psychology rescue surface from the crash notes, but the workbook identifies and matches hypotheses rather than providing one strict focal D/N row per study. Promotion requires selecting a preregistered focal hypothesis, linking it to an exact result statistic, converting the statistic to D, recovering analytic N, and deduplicating against existing Plot 3 psychology corpora. | 0 |

:::

## Specific Observations Included

Machine-readable result-level file: [plot3_preregistered_results.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot3_preregistered_results.csv)

The specific-observation layer has `172` plotted preregistered result rows. It records source citation, result label, journal, `D`, `N`, and the source row number. Support calls are retained in the CSV as metadata but are not used for admission or visual encoding, because they mostly track the preregistered decision rule rather than a distinct effect-size concept.

::: {.dataset-table .specific-observation-table .preregistered-observation-table}

| Source | Specific paper/result | Journal | D/N |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Behavioral intentions (loss vs gain framing) | Affective Science | 0.100 / 15,772 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Policy support: individual autonomy subscale | Affective Science | 0.070 / 15,772 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Policy support: government power subscale | Affective Science | 0.080 / 15,772 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Information seeking (loss vs gain framing) | Affective Science | 0.020 / 15,772 |
| ManyBabies 1 bilingual IDS pooled row [@byersHeinlein2021manybabiesBilingual] | Bilingual infants' infant-directed speech versus adult-directed speech pooled preference e | Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science | 0.260 / 324 |
| ManyBabies 1 IDS pooled row [@manyBabies2020Ids] | Infant-directed speech versus adult-directed speech pooled preference effect | Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science | 0.350 / 2,329 |
| ManyClasses 1 feedback pooled row [@fyfe2021manyclasses1] | Immediate versus delayed feedback timing pooled classroom-learning effect | Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science | 0.002 / 2,081 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H1a: reappraisal versus control reduces negative emotions in response to photos | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.392 / 21,644 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H1b: reappraisal versus control reduces negative state emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.313 / 21,644 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H1c: reappraisal versus control reduces negative emotions about COVID-19 | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.239 / 21,644 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H2a: reappraisal versus control increases positive emotions in response to photos | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.590 / 21,644 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H2b: reappraisal versus control increases positive state emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.326 / 21,644 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H2c: reappraisal versus control increases positive emotions about COVID-19 | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.266 / 21,644 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H3a: reconstrual versus repurposing, negative photo emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.043 / 10,499 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H3b: reconstrual versus repurposing, negative state emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.008 / 10,499 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H3c: reconstrual versus repurposing, negative COVID-19 emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.067 / 10,499 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H4a: repurposing versus reconstrual, positive photo emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.114 / 10,499 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H4b: repurposing versus reconstrual, positive state emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.011 / 10,499 |
| PSA-CR002 cognitive reappraisal hypotheses [@wang2021psacr002] | H4c: repurposing versus reconstrual, positive COVID-19 emotions | Nature Human Behaviour | 0.047 / 10,499 |
| PSA004 true-belief/Gettier pooled row [@hall2024psa004Turri] | Pooled standard justified-true-belief versus Gettier knowledge attribution | Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science | 0.342 / 4,724 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Remotely Close Associations: Openness To Experience And Semantic Memory Structure | European Journal of Personality | 1.290 / 516 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Delaying Access To A Problem-Skipping Option Increases Effortful Practice: Applicat | Computers & Education | 0.074 / 50,433 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - The Morality Of Action: The Asymmetry Between Judgments Of Praise And Blame In The | Journal of Experimental Social Psychology | 0.524 / 59 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - (Pre)Occupations: A Data-Driven Model Of Jobs And Its Consequences For Categorizati | Journal of Experimental Social Psychology | 0.539 / 183 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Ceos Imbue Organizations With Feelings, Increasing Punishment Satisfaction And Apol | Journal of Experimental Social Psychology | 0.290 / 302 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - How Internal Constraints Shape Interest Group Activities: Evidence From Access-Seek | American Political Science Review | 0.004 / 2,647,085 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Treating Depression With Physical Activity In Adolescents And Young Adults: A Syste | Psychological Medicine | 0.820 / 771 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Classified Or Coverup? The Effect Of Redactions On Conspiracy Theory Beliefs | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.103 / 2,524 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - The Effect Of Segregation On Intergroup Relations | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.200 / 1,081 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Preferences For Domestic Action Over International Transfers In Global Climate Poli | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.153 / 1,140 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Do Gender Quotas Really Reduce Bias? Evidence From A Policy Experiment In Southern | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.608 / 51 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2014 - Paying More When Paying For Others. | Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | 0.357 / 151 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - A Functional Analysis Of Two Transdiagnostic, Emotion-Focused Interventions On Nons | Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology | 0.266 / 855 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2015 - The Question–Behavior Effect: Genuine Effect Or Spurious Phenomenon? A Systematic R | Health Psychology | 0.090 / 37,452 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Imagine That! The Effect Of Counterstereotypic Imagined Intergroup Contact On Weigh | Health Psychology | 0.530 / 107 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Being Observed Magnifies Action. | Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | 0.458 / 100 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - Hypocritical Flip-Flop, Or Courageous Evolution? When Leaders Change Their Moral Mi | Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | 0.220 / 5,552 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Selection Bias, Vote Counting, And Money-Priming Effects: A Comment On Rohrer, Pash | Journal of Experimental Psychology: General | 3.129 / 12 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Atheist Horns And Religious Halos: Mental Representations Of Atheists And Theists. | Journal of Experimental Psychology: General | 0.398 / 273 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Experimental Evidence Of Discrimination In The Labour Market: Intersections Between | European Sociological Review | 0.241 / 400 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Worms At Work: Long-Run Impacts Of A Child Health Investment | The Quarterly Journal of Economics | 0.152 / 470 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2013 - Temperament Moderates Associations Between Exposure To Stress And Children's Extern | Child Development | 0.166 / 556 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Inferring Beliefs And Desires From Emotional Reactions To Anticipated And Observed | Child Development | 1.173 / 16 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - When Wanting To Be Fair Is Not Enough: The Effects Of Depletion And Self-Appraisal | Journal of Management | 1.358 / 83 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2015 - Leveling The Playing Field | Psychological Science | 0.396 / 167 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - A Large-Scale Test Of The Goldilocks Hypothesis | Psychological Science | 0.190 / 120,115 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Is Implicit Theory Of Mind A Real And Robust Phenomenon? Results From A Systematic | Psychological Science | 0.580 / 19 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - A Cognitive-Ecological Explanation Of Intergroup Biases | Psychological Science | 0.384 / 209 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Culture-Independent Prerequisites For Early Arithmetic | Psychological Science | 0.678 / 41 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Native Language Promotes Access To Visual Consciousness | Psychological Science | 0.815 / 28 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - ‘Acting Wife’: Marriage Market Incentives And Labor Market Investments | American Economic Review | 0.583 / 60 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Kuppuraj 2018 — Online statistical learning reliability | Royal Society Open Science | 1.800 / 42 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Grubb 2018 — Former targets capture | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 1.100 / 80 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Zanolie 2014 — Magnitude×side Exp3 | Frontiers in Psychology | 1.090 / 20 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Voorspoels 2014 — Coalition encoding + cue | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.850 / 463 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | van ’t Veer 2015 — Liars liked less | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.600 / 155 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Dutilh 2017 — WM drift rate hard | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 0.580 / 916 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | van ’t Veer 2015 — Liars trusted less | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.560 / 155 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Dutilh 2017 — WM drift rate easy | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 0.490 / 916 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rotteveel 2015 — Lever congruency Exp2 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.490 / 50 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Steegen 2014 — Guess averaging immediate | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.400 / 471 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rowland 2014 — RIF Exp2 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.380 / 140 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Ong 2015 — Comfort food MTurk | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.380 / 366 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Verkoeijen 2014 — Spacing inductive learning | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.370 / 123 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Evers 2014 — Diagnosticity Study 1b | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.360 / 199 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rotteveel 2015 — Lever congruency Exp1 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.350 / 99 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Steegen 2014 — Guess averaging delayed | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.340 / 140 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Ong 2015 — Comfort food SG | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.320 / 198 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rowland 2014 — RIF Exp1 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.310 / 72 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | van ’t Veer 2015 — Skin temp observing liar | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.310 / 155 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Kvetnaya 2018 — Irrelevant speech spatial | J European Psych Students | 0.290 / 40 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Bombari 2017 — Body posture felt power | Comprehensive Results in Social Psychology | 0.280 / 200 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Grubb 2018 — Feedback capture diff | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 0.200 / 80 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Voorspoels 2014 — Race encoding + coalition cue | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.180 / 463 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Zanolie 2014 — Magnitude×side Exp2 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.170 / 24 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Jackson 2017 — Posture self-concept size | Comprehensive Results in Social Psychology | 0.150 / 128 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Bombari 2017 — Body posture gambling | Comprehensive Results in Social Psychology | 0.080 / 200 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Zanolie 2014 — Magnitude×side Exp1 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.080 / 20 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Evers 2014 — Diagnosticity Study 1a | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.060 / 253 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rowland 2014 — Cue-independent RIF Exp3 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.050 / 67 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Johnson 2014 — Cleanliness moral judgment Exp1 | Social Psychology | 0.010 / 208 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Johnson 2014 — Cleanliness moral judgment Exp2 | Social Psychology | 0.010 / 126 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Ong 2015 — Comfort food NL | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.010 / 176 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_0) | not coded | 0.408 / 250 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_1) | not coded | 0.080 / 197 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_10) | not coded | 0.408 / 811 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_11) | not coded | 0.170 / 216 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_12) | not coded | 0.494 / 570 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_13) | not coded | 0.050 / 736 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_14) | not coded | 0.480 / 176 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_15) | not coded | 0.201 / 220 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_16) | not coded | 0.221 / 541 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_18) | not coded | 0.905 / 68 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_19) | not coded | 1.291 / 38 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_2) | not coded | 0.059 / 650 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_20) | not coded | 1.640 / 195 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_21) | not coded | 2.726 / 381 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_22) | not coded | 0.247 / 71 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_23) | not coded | 0.370 / 122 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_24) | not coded | 1.540 / 363 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_25) | not coded | 0.262 / 732 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_26) | not coded | 0.090 / 400 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_27) | not coded | 0.120 / 794 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_28) | not coded | 0.080 / 95 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_29) | not coded | 0.773 / 127 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_3) | not coded | 0.926 / 627 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_30) | not coded | 0.480 / 202 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_31) | not coded | 0.191 / 468 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_32) | not coded | 0.667 / 50 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_33) | not coded | 0.060 / 302 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_34) | not coded | 0.040 / 2,141 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_35) | not coded | 0.020 / 2,284 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_36) | not coded | 0.060 / 377 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_38) | not coded | 0.181 / 1,250 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_39) | not coded | 0.020 / 98 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_4) | not coded | 0.747 / 45 |

| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_49) | not coded | 0.201 / 356 |

| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_50) | not coded | 0.327 / 266 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_51) | not coded | 0.120 / 240 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_52) | not coded | 0.290 / 356 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_53) | not coded | 0.937 / 148 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_54) | not coded | 0.221 / 2,053 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_55) | not coded | 0.127 / 1,618 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_56) | not coded | 0.201 / 264 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_57) | not coded | 0.470 / 81 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_58) | not coded | 0.352 / 374 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_6) | not coded | 0.080 / 55 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_60) | not coded | 0.414 / 272 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_61) | not coded | 0.279 / 205 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_62) | not coded | 0.270 / 268 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_63) | not coded | 1.031 / 545 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_64) | not coded | 0.340 / 87 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_65) | not coded | 0.360 / 252 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_66) | not coded | 0.703 / 177 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_67) | not coded | 0.797 / 101 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_68) | not coded | 0.800 / 379 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_69) | not coded | 0.590 / 297 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_7) | not coded | 0.408 / 905 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_70) | not coded | 0.140 / 1,673 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_72) | not coded | 0.837 / 51 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_73) | not coded | 1.341 / 37 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_74) | not coded | 0.230 / 80 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_75) | not coded | 0.286 / 400 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_76) | not coded | 0.180 / 120,115 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_77) | not coded | 0.494 / 621 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_78) | not coded | 1.341 / 37 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_79) | not coded | 0.540 / 332 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_8) | not coded | 0.160 / 680 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_80) | not coded | 0.030 / 386 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_81) | not coded | 0.240 / 53 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_82) | not coded | 2.630 / 1,462 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_83) | not coded | 0.090 / 242 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_84) | not coded | 0.220 / 199 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_85) | not coded | 0.470 / 471 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_86) | not coded | 0.181 / 463 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_87) | not coded | 1.500 / 90 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_88) | not coded | 0.629 / 36 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_89) | not coded | 0.190 / 631 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_9) | not coded | 0.606 / 257 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_90) | not coded | 1.372 / 20 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_91) | not coded | 0.030 / 1,894 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_92) | not coded | 1.155 / 79 |

:::

### What Is Still Missing

- The included core now combines Registered Reports, PSA-CR001 and PSA-CR002 pooled/preregistered hypothesis rows, PSA004 pooled Gettier evidence, ManyBabies 1 and ManyBabies 1 Bilingual, ManyClasses 1, the Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered key-effect sample, and SCORE preregistration-indicated paper-level rows.
- The expanded considered-but-not-included list now names the major local preregistered-like sidecars separately: Many Labs, RRR pair rows, PSA replication rows, Transparent Psi, RPCB Registered Report replication effects, Allen/Mehler RR support-rate evidence, ManyBabies 3, ManyBabies 4, ManyClasses 2, EGAP Metaketa I/III/IV native ATE rows, ERN/Pe, self-control fMRI, Twomey, Linden, Protzko, AACT/ClinicalTrials.gov, CliniFact, Brodeur preregistered/PAP economics table tests, Nordic trial reporting, FReD, communication privacy, retrieval-extinction rats, the van den Akker preregistration-in-practice corpus, and the larger van den Akker selective-hypothesis-reporting corpus.
- Most of those extra local sources are out because they are already Plot 1 replication-pair evidence, registry metadata rather than analytic preregistration, publication-linkage metadata without effect statistics, document/native-metric payloads without a compact D/N result table, or extracted table-test corpora without a focal/main-result selector.
- The considered list is still a working audit, not a claim that every preregistered-like source on the web has been exhausted.
